name: pressure_fed_lox_kerosene_system_bb12512d
description: Design a pressure-fed rocket engine feed system using LOX (liquid oxygen) and kerosene as propellants, with nitrogen (GN2) as the pressurant gas. The system must include: a GN2 pressurant tank, a LOX tank, a kerosene tank, a rocket engine block, pressurization lines from GN2 to both propellant tanks, and feed lines from each tank to the engine. The design_seed "pressure_fed_lox_kerosene" provides the baseline architecture. FIXED: component architecture (gn2_tank, lox_tank, kerosene_tank, engine, gn2_to_lox_pressurization, gn2_to_kerosene_pressurization, lox_feed_line, kerosene_feed_line). TUNABLE: tank pressures, volumes, initial masses, line diameters, and flow rates. The system must sustain simultaneous propellant flow to the engine with proper pressurization.

notes: Use pressure_fed_lox_kerosene seed for baseline component names and topology
Ensure both propellant feed lines deliver flow to engine simultaneously
Pressurization lines must maintain tank pressure above engine inlet requirements
Preserve the Engine node and the two feed Series connections into it.
Tune feed CdA values before changing topology.
If physical warnings mention a component, fix that component first.

checks.description: Simulation must complete without crashing or invalid configuration
No nonphysical or degenerate warnings during simulation
LOX feed line must deliver propellant to engine (nonzero mass flow)
Kerosene feed line must deliver propellant to engine (nonzero mass flow)
GN2 pressurization line to LOX tank must deliver flow (nonzero mass flow)
GN2 pressurization line to kerosene tank must deliver flow (nonzero mass flow)
LOX tank pressure must remain positive throughout simulation
Kerosene tank pressure must remain positive throughout simulation
GN2 tank pressure must remain positive throughout simulation
Engine chamber pressure must remain positive throughout simulation
LOX feed line delivers non-zero mass flow to the engine.
Kerosene feed line delivers non-zero mass flow to the engine.
